page: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine instance belonging to the Dnlv6 family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores built on the x86_64 architecture. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations.

The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This low-memory design represents a resource density tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute power over memory capacity. Additionally, the instance does not include local storage, requiring the attachment of remote Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across several compute and memory metrics. The instance achieved a Passmark CPU score of 56283.8, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware, with similar strong results in encryption and physics tests. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reached 252.33 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reached 104.24 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. In LLM inference benchmarks using a small 135M model, the instance demonstrated top-tier text generation speeds of 337.85 tokens/sec, alongside strong prompt processing speeds across medium and large models.

Given its high compute density, advanced vector and matrix instruction support, and network optimization, the D48nls_v6 offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, network-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk storage. It is suitable for tasks such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, cryptographic processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration results in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and lacks local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU tests, and static web serving throughput. It also demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for small-model LLM inference. The D48nls_v6 is suited for compute-intensive, network-bound workloads, multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong performance in compute, cryptography, and LLM inference benchmarks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48nls_v6 is a network-optimized instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and strong compute and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A compute-dense, network-optimized virtual machine featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and strong memory bandwidth for CPU-intensive processing tasks.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
96.0 GB system memory providing a compute-dense 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage, requiring remote Premium Storage attachment
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong benchmark performance in memory bandwidth, cryptography, and static web serving
Well-suited for CPU-based LLM inference, multi-threaded decompression, and network-bound workloads
